common_models.name: Minimum-cost network flow / transportation assignment LP: decision variable x_ij = number of refugees moved from source A_i (5 origin countries) to sink B_j (11 receiving countries); supply constraints sum_j x_ij = n_i, capacity (ceiling) constraints sum_i x_ij <= m_j

Floyd-Warshall all-pairs shortest path to convert adjacent-node transport accessibility into the least-accessibility-cost matrix d_ij between every origin and receiving country over the abstracted route graph

Accessibility Index as a custom edge weight: A_ij = T_ij / (1 - alpha*D_ij), combining transport time T and route death rate D with a risk-appetite parameter alpha (set =1), constrained by alpha*D_ij < 1

Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to compress 5 receiving-country metrics (GDP per capita, cereal production, pension spending, healthcare spending, doctors per 1000) into a single 'living quality index' c_j; first PC Y1 retained 81.2% of variance and the explicit linear loading equation is reported (c_i(0) = -4.286 + 0.515 z1 + 0.132 z2 + 4.403e-5 z3 + 0.1 z4 + 0.24 z5)
Weighted-sum multi-objective model (Model 1): min w1*sum(x_ij*d_ij') + w2*sum(x_ij*c_j') after standardizing both objectives, with sum w_i = 1

Goal Programming model (Model 2): preemptive/priority goal programming that first computes single-objective goal values g1 (min accessibility cost) and g2 (max living quality), then minimizes weighted deviations p1*e1+ + p2*e2- with transport accessibility set as the first-priority level

System Dynamics / iterative dynamic model: time-stepped (monthly, t=0..12, 11 iterations) re-optimization where living-quality c_i(t) degrades as refugees pour in via per-capita dilution z_1i(t)=z_1i(0)/(1+ (m_i(t)/M_i)*1.5) plus an added penalty function f(m_i(t)) capturing social friction; flow-conservation constraints carry refugee stocks between European countries each step
Spearman rank correlation (rho ≈ 0.6) used as a validation device to show receiving-country capacity correlates with observed arrivals
Capacity ceiling rule M_i = 0.5% of national population as the hard upper bound on refugees a country can receive (later also referenced as a 10% variant)
Gravity Model (Newtonian I = P_i*P_j / D_ij^a) adapted into a comprehensive node attraction/weight index M_ij^t
I. S. Lowry regression migration model (job-opportunity-driven migration) cited as inspiration for the weight formulation
Hierarchical cluster analysis on average node-to-startpoint distances (matrix D, height values K1/K2/K3) to partition 11 origin nodes into 3 distance layers
Network/graph node model with layered directed flow (initial-layer -> propagation-layer -> terminal-layer -> 6 route start points)
Cellular Automaton dynamic simulation of period-by-period (quarterly) migration with feedback (capacity saturation, weight re-normalization)
Cobweb Model relating node pass-rate P to passed population N to argue capacity-vs-stability for the policy recommendation

common_techniques.name: Two-layer modeling: a graph-theoretic layer (Floyd-Warshall on the route network) feeds a clean LP transportation layer, so the messy geography is pre-collapsed into a d_ij cost matrix before optimization

Index engineering: every qualitative factor (route danger, living quality) is turned into a single scalar — Accessibility Index fuses time+death rate, PCA fuses 5 welfare metrics — so a multi-attribute problem reduces to two clean objectives
Handling conflicting objectives two independent ways (weighted-sum AND goal programming) and comparing the resulting allocations, which doubles as a robustness/sensitivity check
Abstraction of the real map: 5 representative origin nodes A1-A5 chosen by refugee volume, 11 major receiving nodes B1-B11, edges weighted by accessibility not geographic distance

Making a static optimization dynamic by re-solving on a monthly rolling horizon with state carried forward (refugee stocks) and parameters (living quality) updated endogenously via resource-dilution + penalty terms — turns one LP into a 12-step simulation

Direct parameter-perturbation for scenario analysis: model exogenous shocks (Hungary-Austria border closure, Belgium capacity -30%, terror attacks) by editing specific d_ij/c_j entries and re-running, then reporting the objective-value jump (2.41e8 -> 3.44e8) as evidence of impact

Tight model-to-policy traceability: each of the 5 policy recommendations is justified by which model parameter it moves (sea patrol -> lowers death rate D -> lowers A_ij; infrastructure -> raises c_j; border cooperation -> preserves d_ij connectivity)

Iterative model-refinement narrative: started with a random/equal-weight migration model, showed it FAILED to match data (R=0.6543, p>0.05), then explicitly revised it with the Gravity model (R=0.9914), then added Cellular Automaton feedback (R=1.0). The refutation of their own first model is a deliberate persuasion device.
Layered abstraction of geography into nodes whose resolution is tunable to a policymaker's jurisdiction (county/state/nation) - directly ties the model to the policy audience

Compositing many qualitative policy factors (safety, environmental/cultural acceptance, transport convenience, repatriation ratio, entry-point count) into one log-linear weight: ln M_ij = ln S + ln Conv - ln B + K_a
Quantitative validation at every stage using correlation coefficient R and significance p against real 2014 IOM/UNHCR/Eurostat/FRONTEX-FRAN route-share data

Encoding shocks as toggle/indicator variables: war beta in {0,1}, weather gamma in {-2c,-c,0,c}, sea-vs-land crossing x1/x2, so exogenous events (disease, terrorism, GO/NGO funding +/-0.2) plug into the same safety/weight equations
Sensitivity analysis quantified as elasticity bands: +/-5% capacity -> +/-2% output, >+/-10% capacity -> >4.7% output

Honest scalability stress test: applied model to USA/Canada/China, got absurd results (7 refugees to Canada), and used the FAILURE to surface a real modeling insight (planimetric distance ignores the Himalayas and Atlantic barriers)
Multi-tool implementation: MATLAB for static weight simulation, C++ for the cellular-automaton dynamics, JavaScript for animated visualization of flows (circle-area = population)

recurring_pitfalls.name: Heavy reliance on the M_i = 0.5%-of-population ceiling, and the model explicitly cannot allocate the overflow once demand exceeds total capacity — exactly the failure mode the 10x-scalability sub-question probes, which they acknowledge but do not solve

Internal inconsistency / data hygiene issues: Table 2's 'total' equals A1's value (14078) not the actual sum; result tables (Table 4 vs Table 5) are both captioned 'Model 1'; several B_j row totals in the goal-programming table don't match the cell sum (e.g., B3, B7), and the rendered map/figure tables are garbled (repeated 'Finland', 'French Republic (North)') — sloppy presentation that could cost polish points

The risk-appetite parameter alpha is set to 1 for all refugees with no calibration or sensitivity sweep, and the Accessibility Index form A=T/(1-alpha*D) is asserted rather than derived from behavior data

The dynamic model's key magic numbers — replacing the benign 0.5% dilution with a *1.5 factor and adding an unspecified penalty function f(m_i(t)) — are introduced with hand-wavy 'system dynamics estimated' justification and no actual SD stock-flow diagram or equations shown, so the 'System Dynamics Model' label is somewhat aspirational

Self-admitted unmodeled drivers: country policy, cultural/religious acceptance, language, and NGO/UNHCR effects are all excluded from the quantitative model even though they dominate the real crisis and the policy section leans on them

PCA living-quality index is computed once from 2013 data and frozen; refugees-as-rational-utility-maximizers assumption (Assumption 1) ignores family/network herding effects that the literature (their own cited refs) says matter
Exogenous-event analysis is a few one-off perturbations (one border pair, one -30% capacity cut) rather than a systematic distributional or worst-case robustness study
Distances are straight-line (planimetric) - the model ignores geographic barriers, which the team admits broke the USA/Canada/China scalability test (Himalayas, Atlantic)
EB (Eastern Borders) route and its nodes were dropped entirely because all sampled origins are in Africa/Middle East, so a whole real migration channel is excluded

Several formulas are under-specified or contain typos/inconsistencies (e.g., the entry-point Bernoulli P(x=n_i) cases, the changed-weight expression w'_i2 = pi*W_i2, the M_ij factor list f(...) vs the final ln M_ij dropping C_ij and Point terms) - dimensional/notational rigor is loose
Many coefficients (danger coefficients c1-c4, adjusting coefficients, GO/NGO impact range +/-0.2, weather constant c) are asserted without derivation or calibration source
Claims like R=1.0000 are implausibly perfect and suggest overfitting to a tiny number of route-share points rather than genuine predictive validation

OCR/text artifacts aside, the model conflates 'optimal route for refugees' with 'observed route shares' - it validates against where refugees DID go, which weakens the normative 'optimal policy' claim
Redistribution of refugees after entering Europe and illegal stowaways are omitted, and the team acknowledges the data for these is missing
The Cobweb-model policy argument is asserted qualitatively from two schematic figures rather than simulated, so the central capacity-stability policy claim is the least rigorously supported part

reusable_patterns.name: Index-then-optimize pipeline: collapse multi-attribute edge costs and node desirabilities into single scalars (a fused safety/time index for edges, a PCA composite for nodes) so any messy real problem becomes a clean LP/network problem the judges recognize

Use Floyd-Warshall (or any APSP) as a preprocessing step to turn a sparse adjacency-weighted map into a dense origin-to-destination cost matrix that an assignment/transportation LP can consume directly
Solve a multi-objective trade-off two ways (weighted-sum after standardization, and priority goal programming) and present both — instant built-in robustness narrative and shows methodological range

PCA-as-justification template: report the % variance captured by PC1 (here 81.2%) and the explicit loading equation to defend reducing N correlated indicators to one composite, then reuse those loadings to make the composite time-varying

Static -> dynamic upgrade by rolling-horizon re-optimization: re-solve the LP each period, carry state (stocks) forward via flow-conservation constraints, and let parameters degrade endogenously (per-capita resource dilution + a congestion/social penalty) — a cheap, defensible way to add 'systemic dynamics'
Capacity-ceiling-as-percentage-of-population as a simple, policy-grounded hard constraint (cite that the EU itself used population-based quotas)

Scenario analysis by direct parameter surgery: model a shock by editing the specific matrix entries it would change (cut an edge, shrink a capacity) and report the resulting objective-value delta as quantitative evidence of impact

Model-to-policy traceability matrix: write each policy recommendation as 'lever X changes parameter Y which improves objective Z' so the recommendations are provably grounded in the model rather than generic — and frame the deliverable in the required format (UN letter)
Validate a key modeling assumption against real data with a lightweight nonparametric stat (Spearman rho between modeled capacity and observed arrivals) instead of leaving it asserted

The 'build-it, break-it, fix-it' validation arc: deliberately present a naive baseline model, quantitatively show it fails (report low R / high p), then motivate each refinement with the gap to data. This turns model selection into a persuasive story judges love.

Composite-index pattern: fold many incommensurable qualitative factors into a single log-linear weight derived from a named classical model (here Gravity), so policy concerns become tractable math while staying interpretable.
Tunable-resolution abstraction: define your unit (node) so its granularity can be set by the decision-maker's jurisdiction - an easy, high-value move for any policy problem.
Layered network + cellular-automaton + feedback as a general template for any spatial diffusion/flow problem over time with capacity limits and saturation thresholds.

Encode shocks/scenarios as indicator and toggle variables (war 0/1, weather levels, +/-20% capacity) plugged into the SAME core equations, so 'exogenous events' and 'sensitivity' sections reuse one model instead of new ones.
Report a single headline correlation R (and p) at each modeling stage and put the climbing sequence in the summary sheet - a compact, judge-friendly proof of progress.
